frame_analyses.description: Title slide: 'Suturing' with credits to Elaine Cole (Senior Lecturer in Emergency Care, City University London) and Natasa Perovic (CETL Learning technologist)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational content production by experienced instructors

frame_analyses.description: Needle visible with pink foam block (suture site marker). Left hand positioning for needle guidance. Demonstration setup on surgical mat.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear visibility of suture entry point on marked foam block

frame_analyses.description: Needle driver grasping needle in preparation for drive through foam. Two instruments positioned for suture placement. Right hand needle driver, left hand stabilizing foam.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates proper needle grip position for backhand suture driving

frame_analyses.description: Needle being driven through foam block. Clear entry point visible on marked site. Both hands coordinated for needle trajectory control.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Perpendicular needle entry angle demonstrated

frame_analyses.description: Exit of needle from opposite side of foam block. Suture line visible across foam. Instrument positioning shows controlled technique.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean entry and exit points shown

frame_analyses.description: Suture positioned with both ends free. Needle drivers repositioned for first throw (surgeon's knot). Right hand leading position observable.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup for double throw demonstrated

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver wrapping suture around left driver jaw. First component of surgeon's knot (double throw) in progress. Clear hand positioning visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates proper wrapping technique for double throw with right hand leading

frame_analyses.description: Tension applied to complete first wrap. Both drivers held at controlled distance. Knot formation visible around suture.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth tension application shown

frame_analyses.description: Second wrap of surgeon's knot being formed. Right hand continuing wrapping motion. Suture loop formation clearly visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double throw completion demonstrated

frame_analyses.description: First throw (surgeon's knot) complete. Knot visible on suture. Right hand leads confirmed. Preparation for second throw with hand switch.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Completed surgeon's knot shows appropriate tension and security

frame_analyses.description: Instruments repositioned for second throw. Left hand now leading (switched from right). Single throw sequence beginning.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch from first throw clearly demonstrated

frame_analyses.description: Left hand driver wrapping suture around right driver. Single throw formation. Alternating hand technique from first throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean single throw with appropriate hand switch

frame_analyses.description: Second throw complete. Knot stack visible on suture. Preparation for third throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Second throw shows security and proper tension

frame_analyses.description: Instruments repositioned for third throw. Right hand now leading (switched from left). Single throw sequence.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch demonstrated again for third throw

frame_analyses.description: Right hand driver wrapping suture for third throw. Single wrap formation visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Consistent hand alternation technique

frame_analyses.description: Third throw complete. All three throws (surgeon's knot + 2 single throws) stacked on suture. Knot appears secure.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Complete knot sequence demonstrated

frame_analyses.description: Completed knot on foam block. Suture ends visible. Knot positioned on marked site. No gap visible in foam.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final knot placement shows no drain damage or misalignment

frame_analyses.description: Text overlay: 'Suturing technique' and 'one more stitch...' indicating continuation or additional teaching content.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational narration suggests this is instructional demonstration

frame_analyses.description: Alternative camera angle (light teal surgical mat). Pink foam block with suture visible. Different hand positioning shown from new perspective.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates technique from alternative viewpoint

frame_analyses.description: Close-up view showing needle driver hand positioning and foam block interaction. Demonstrates proper ergonomic hand stance.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational detail of proper hand positioning during suture driving

frame_analyses.description: Close-up of knot formation with both hands visible. Shows precision grip on needle drivers during knot tying.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational focus on hand coordination during knot manipulation

frame_analyses.description: Completed multi-throw knot visible on suture in foam block. No gap, no avulsion. Knot appears secure and properly positioned.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final assessment shows successful task completion in demonstration

video_type_determination: This is an instructional/educational video, not a performance video for scoring.
reason_not_scored: This video is classified as 'instructional' content. It features educational narration (title credits from Elaine Cole and Natasa Perovic), multiple camera angles for teaching purposes, and an overlay text ('Suturing technique - one more stitch...') that indicates instructional intent rather than a trainee/surgeon performance assessment. Per FLS scoring guidelines, instructional videos with narration, overlays, and educational setup are not scored for performance metrics but analyzed for teaching content instead.

demonstrated_techniques: Proper needle driver grip positioning at 2/3 point from needle tip
Perpendicular needle entry angle through foam block suture sites
Surgeon's knot (double throw) formation with right hand leading
Single throw alternating hand technique (throws 2 and 3)
Precise hand-to-hand coordination during knot manipulation
Appropriate tension application during throw completion
Suture placement accuracy on marked entry/exit points
Knot stacking for security without excessive bulk
verbal_cues: Overlay text: 'Suturing technique' and 'one more stitch...' — indicating progression through technique teaching
common_errors_addressed: Demonstrates avoiding oblique needle entry (perpendicular angle shown throughout)
Shows proper hand switching between throws (explicit demonstration of alternating left/right lead)
Avoids loose knots by maintaining consistent tension
Prevents drain/foam damage through controlled knot formation
Demonstrates clean suture placement within marked boundaries
teaching_content.pedagogical_intent: Educational demonstration of intracorporeal suturing technique for training purposes. Multiple camera angles and close-up views optimize visibility for learners. Structured progression through all phases of the task (needle load, suture placement, three-throw knot sequence).

created_by: Elaine Cole (Senior Lecturer in Emergency Care, City University London)

video_metadata.audience: FLS trainees and medical students learning intracorporeal suturing

summary: This is an expert-level instructional video demonstrating correct intracorporeal suturing technique. It is not a performance recording and therefore does not receive an FLS score. The video effectively teaches proper technique through multiple angles, clear hand positioning, and systematic progression through all knot throws. Teaching content is comprehensive and appropriate for novice-to-intermediate learners.
